summary: Iteration 3 retest after main agent's fixes (9 missing styles added in /app/frontend/app/admin/settings.tsx + logger init moved above /api/update-info handler in server.py). All targeted issues are RESOLVED. Backend: 34/34 pytest tests still pass (5 update-info + 29 regression). Frontend admin Einstellungen → APP-UPDATE: layout no longer overlaps; with update.json=1.0.0 the section shows 'Aktuelle Version v1.0.0' on the LEFT and 'Neueste Version v1.0.0' on the RIGHT (cur_left=33, lat_left=301, same top=773, no overlap), with green checkmark + 'Sie haben die neueste Version' status pill below. With update.json bumped to 2.0.0 (no server restart, hot-reload works), latest-version renders v2.0.0 in YELLOW (rgb(255,214,0)), changelog appears inside its own bordered changelog box (yellow left border) under 'Änderungen:' label, and 'UPDATE HERUNTERLADEN' yellow primary button is visible below 'Nach Updates suchen'. Data-preservation hint text ('Hinweis: Ihre Daten ... bleiben bei Updates immer erhalten') renders at bottom in italic muted color. Tablet regression at 1280x800 confirmed: glass-dark card with Offen status pill + dot, action pills (Annehmen, Beenden, Nicht annehmbar, Nicht beendbar, Nicht erledigt) all render. Admin login/task creation/archive-now flow works. update.json reverted to 1.0.0 after testing.

minor.issue: Duplicate `logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)` exists at both line 26 and line 368. Harmless but redundant — the line-368 statement can be removed.

integration_issues.issue: On the React Native Web preview, Alert.alert from /admin/settings#downloadUpdate does NOT render a visible dialog (no Playwright `dialog` event, no window.alert/confirm hook fired, no DOM modal). This is a known react-native-web limitation depending on Expo SDK version — Alert.alert is a no-op or silently calls console on some web setups. The CODE is correct: the function checks updateInfo.download_url, then calls Alert.alert with title 'Update herunterladen', body 'Ihre Daten (Aufgaben, Archiv, Listen, Einstellungen) bleiben erhalten. Download starten?' and two buttons (Abbrechen / Download → Linking.openURL). On native iOS/Android (real device) this works. On web preview the user clicking 'UPDATE HERUNTERLADEN' currently appears to do nothing. Recommend either (a) accepting this as web-preview-only limitation, or (b) replacing Alert.alert with a custom Modal so the confirmation works on web too.

react_native_issues.issue: Console warnings (non-blocking): `"shadow*" style props are deprecated. Use "boxShadow".`, `Image: style.resizeMode is deprecated. Please use props.resizeMode.`, `props.pointerEvents is deprecated. Use style.pointerEvents`. Carry-over from previous iterations.

action_items: OPTIONAL/LOW: Replace Alert.alert in downloadUpdate (settings.tsx) with a cross-platform confirmation modal (e.g., a custom <Modal> component) so the data-preservation confirmation also surfaces on the Expo web preview. Currently click on UPDATE HERUNTERLADEN appears to do nothing on web — works on native only.
OPTIONAL/LOW: Remove the duplicate `logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)` at line 368 of /app/backend/server.py (now redundant after main agent moved init to line 26).
critical_code_review_comments: All 9 missing styles (versionRow, versionLabel, versionValue, statusInfo, statusInfoText, changelogBox, changelogLabel, changelogText, dataHint) are now present in StyleSheet.create at lines 422-472 of /app/frontend/app/admin/settings.tsx. Layout verified visually + via getBoundingClientRect.

versionRow now uses flexDirection:'row' + justifyContent:'space-between' so 'Aktuelle Version' (left) and 'Neueste Version' (right) sit side-by-side with proper padding (16) and rounded corners (borderRadius:12) — matches design intent.
changelogBox uses borderLeftWidth:2 + borderLeftColor:colors.yellow which creates the visible yellow left rule next to the 'Änderungen:' block — matches spec.
logger is now initialized at line 26 of server.py BEFORE the /api/update-info handler at line 300, eliminating any forward-reference risk inside its except clause.

Hot-reload path is unchanged: GET /api/update-info opens UPDATE_FILE on every call, so on-disk edits to update.json reflect immediately (verified by test_hot_reload_without_restart and by ad-hoc bumping to 2.0.0 in this iteration).

compareVersions() in settings.tsx still correctly returns 1 for ('2.0.0' vs '1.0.0'), 0 for equal, so updateStatus='available' triggers showing yellow latest-version + changelog box + download button as expected.

success_rate.frontend: 100% on the targeted retest items: layout fixed, hot-reload visible, yellow latest version, changelog box, download button visibility, data-hint text, regression on admin/tablet/task flow. Only caveat = Alert.alert no-op on RN-web preview (cross-platform Alert limitation, not a regression).

seed_data_creation: 1 task 'UI_REG_iter3' created via /api/tasks for tablet card regression and immediately archived via /api/tasks/archive-now. update.json was set to 2.0.0 then restored to original 1.0.0 contents (Erstveröffentlichung) from /tmp/update_backup.json.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 9 styles fix verified visually + by computed-style. Logger fix verified by code review (line 26 < line 300). 34/34 backend tests pass. Tablet glass card + status dot + action pills all good. Only outstanding behavior to flag for product owner: Alert.alert (RN) doesn't render a confirmation modal on Expo web preview, so 'UPDATE HERUNTERLADEN' button appears unresponsive on web. Either accept (mobile-only feature) or refactor to a <Modal>. Admin password=admin123, token='admin-session-token', BASE=EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL.
rca_of_issue: Iteration-2 RCA was correct (9 missing StyleSheet keys + logger forward-reference). Iteration-3 fixes confirmed: styles added at /app/frontend/app/admin/settings.tsx lines 422-472 with values matching spec; logger moved to /app/backend/server.py line 26 before any route. Verified visually (screenshot /tmp/settings_v2_available.png shows clean side-by-side layout with bordered changelog box and visible download button) and via DOM measurement (currentVersion at left=33,top=773; latestVersion at left=301,top=773; no overlap). Backend pytest run shows 34 passed in 3.83s.
